note: Sprint 5.14: adds Useful Compute reward budget policy v0.1. Caps governance-approved rewards by pool, daily, epoch, job and worker limits before any payment sprint. No automatic payout, no wallet access, no broadcast, no consensus. New scripts/trinity/useful_compute_reward_budget_policy.py reads governance batches and emits a deterministic budget plan; defaults are conservative (max_daily_fraction 0.0001 + fixed cap 1 SOST, max_epoch_fraction 0.001 + fixed cap 10 SOST, max_job 0.05 SOST, max_worker 0.02 SOST). Allocation runs the cap stack worker then job then epoch then daily and labels each row approved_as_requested/capped_by_worker/capped_by_job/capped_by_epoch/capped_by_daily/deferred/rejected. Each allocated item is split 70 percent primary_workers / 20 percent replay_validator_reserve / 10 percent governance_review_reserve and the three sub-totals always sum exactly to allocated_stocks. budget_id is sha16(canonical(policy, pinned_time, epoch_id, pool, caps, allocations)) so two runs on the same inputs are byte-identical. New schemas/trinity/useful_compute_reward_budget.schema.json (strict, additionalProperties false, six const-true safety flags including budget_only and requires_separate_payment_sprint). website/trinity-useful-compute.html gains a Reward Budget Policy card plus three new counters (governance_approved_stocks, budget_allocated_stocks, budget_deferred_stocks). Badge bumped to v0.8. Tests: 48 new across tests/trinity/test_{useful_compute_reward_budget_policy,useful_compute_reward_budget_schema,reward_budget_web_console}.py; static safety + no-network checks extended to cover useful_compute_reward_budget_policy.py. Trinity suite 544/544 PASS with materials-engine-private present. docs/TRINITY_USEFUL_COMPUTE_REWARD_BUDGET_POLICY_V01.md. No consensus, RPC, node, miner, wallet or wire-format change.
